chunks.text: Course: CS 294 | EECS at UC Berkeley CS 294. Special Topics Catalog Description: Topics will vary from semester to semester. See Computer Science Division announcements. Units: 1-4 Also Offered As: COMPSCI 294 Formats: Fall: 2.0-5.0 hours of lecture per week Fall: 3.0-15.0 hours of lecture per week Spring: 3.0-15.0 hours of lecture per week Fall: 3.0-9.0 hours of lecture per week Spring: 3.0-9.0 hours of lecture per week Spring: 2.0-6.0 hours of lecture per week Fall: 2.0-6.0 hours of lecture per week Fall: 1.0-3.0 hours of lecture per week Spring: 1.0-3.0 hours of lecture per week Spring: 2.0-5.0 hours of lecture per week Grading Basis: Student Option Final Exam Status: No Class Schedule (Spring 2026): CS 294-184 – WeFr 09:00-10:29, Soda 405 – S. E. Chasins Class Notes No time conflicts allowed CS 294-194 – Th 17:00-18:29, Soda 310 – Ali Ghodsi , Ion Stoica , Joseph E. Gonzalez , Kurt W Keutzer , Prabal Dutta , Trevor Darrell CS 294-258 – Mo 14:00-16:59, Etcheverry 3105 – Alane Suhr Class Notes - Recommended prerequisites: EECS 183/283A (should have exposure to NLP, RL, as well as intro ML/AI, potentially some PL background as well) - Time conflicts are not allowed - No final exam CS 294-265 – Mo 09:00-11:59, Soda 310 – Ion Stoica Class Notes Prerequisites: CS 262A or CS 268 CS 294-268 – Fr 13:00-14:59, Soda 320 – Venkatesan Guruswami Class Notes - Prerequisites: None for PhD students. For undergraduates or MS students, mathematical maturity and instructor permission.

For undergraduates or MS students, mathematical maturity and instructor permission. - No time conflicts - No final exam CS 194-271/294-271 – Tu 12:30-13:59, Soda 606 – Gireeja Vishnu Ranade , Narges Norouzi Class Notes Application to enroll: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdAkh4uCiQdYyfO4Df-6Nxwv36ZTtj6ex-HdloiQdy0f8H5gA/viewform CS 294-273 – Th 09:00-11:59, Soda 320 – Jonathan Stray Class Notes * Time conflicts are NOT allowed. CS 294-290 – Mo 13:00-15:59, Soda 310 – Benjamin Recht Class Notes - Prerequisites: A background in control (EE 221A or equivalent) and machine learning (CS 281A or equivalent) CS 294-304 – TuTh 14:00-15:29, Jacobs Hall 310 – Bjoern Hartmann Class Notes - Prerequisites: Suggested background in either HCI or AI - No time conflicts - An alternate final will be offered Links: EECS Course Archives (CalNet authentication required) CS enrollment policies EE enrollment policies HKN course maps
